title: Demjanov and Tiffeneau-Demjanov Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Demjanov and Tiffeneau-Demjanov rearrangements describing nitrous-acid-mediated ring expansion of aminomethylcycloalkanes or 1-aminomethylcycloalkanols to the corresponding cycloalkanol or cycloalkanone homologues through diazonium-ion formation and 1,2-alkyl migration.
notes: Key concepts: nitrosation to diazonium ions, ring expansion by 1,2-alkyl shift, Demjanov versus Tiffeneau-Demjanov substrate classes, competitive rearrangement pathways.

title: Demjanov and Tiffeneau-Demjanov Rearrangement – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Demjanov and Tiffeneau-Demjanov rearrangements featuring ring expansion in syntheses of homobrexan-2-one, homospectinomycin analogues, and 2-aminomethyl-7-oxabicyclo[2.2.1]heptane derivatives under nitrosation conditions.
notes: Examples emphasize aminoalcohol preparation, nitrous-acid-mediated rearrangement, and stereochemical control in strained bicyclic systems.

title: Dess-Martin Oxidation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Dess-Martin oxidation describing Dess-Martin periodinane (DMP), a soluble hypervalent iodine(V) reagent derived from IBX, as a mild and selective oxidant for 1° and 2° alcohols and oximes to the corresponding carbonyl compounds.
notes: Key concepts: IBX to DMP reagent preparation, hypervalent iodine oxidation, mild neutral conditions, aldehyde/ketone formation, alkoxyperiodinane intermediates.

title: Dess-Martin Oxidation – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Dess-Martin oxidation featuring late-stage oxidations in syntheses of ustiloxin D, a CP-molecule gamma-hydroxy lactone core, and the E-F fragment of (+)-spongistatin-2, including one-pot oxidation and cascade oxidation variants.
notes: Examples emphasize sensitive polyoxygenated substrates, sequential oxidation sequences, and compatibility with advanced natural-product intermediates.

title: Dieckmann Condensation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Dieckmann condensation describing intramolecular Claisen condensation of diesters to cyclic beta-keto esters and the related Thorpe-Ziegler cyclization of dinitriles to enamino nitriles under strong base.
notes: Key concepts: intramolecular Claisen condensation, cyclic beta-keto ester formation, Thorpe-Ziegler cyclization, reversible enolate-driven ring closure, ring-size effects.

title: Dieckmann Condensation – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Dieckmann condensation featuring aromatic annulation en route to mycophenolic acid, macro-Dieckmann cyclization in (-)-galbonolide B synthesis, and intramolecular Dieckmann closure during (+)-sacacarin construction.
notes: Examples highlight kinetic-versus-thermodynamic enolate behavior, macrocyclization under high dilution, and tandem annulation strategies.

title: Diels-Alder Cycloaddition – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Diels-Alder cycloaddition describing the concerted [4+2] cycloaddition of dienes with alkenes or alkynes to form cyclohexene derivatives, including normal and inverse electron-demand modes, regioselectivity, endo preference, and stereospecificity.
notes: Key concepts: concerted pericyclic reaction, endo rule, normal versus inverse electron-demand Diels-Alder reactivity, ortho/para regiocontrol, aromatic transition state.

title: Diels-Alder Cycloaddition – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Diels-Alder cycloaddition featuring intermolecular and chiral Lewis-acid-catalyzed cycloadditions in syntheses of asatone, rubrolone aglycon, eunicenone A, and (+)-rishirilide B.
notes: Examples emphasize powerful complexity generation, complete diastereoselectivity, and catalyst-controlled endo/exo selectivity in complex natural-product settings.

title: Dienone-Phenol Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the dienone-phenol rearrangement describing acid- or base-promoted migration of alkyl groups in cyclohexadienones to aromatic phenols, including ortho- and para-dienone cases and sequential 1,2-shift pathways that deliver highly substituted phenols.
notes: Key concepts: dienone rearomatization, alkyl migration, ortho versus para substitution patterns, 1,2-shift cascades, acid-catalyzed rearrangement of santonin-type frameworks.

title: Dienone-Phenol Rearrangement – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the dienone-phenol rearrangement featuring photochemical access to tetra- and pentasubstituted phenols, rearrangement of phenolic bis C-aryl glycosides en route to kidamycin models, and condition-dependent O- versus C-migration of spirodienones.
notes: Examples emphasize photochemical dienone-phenol rearrangement, Lewis-acid-mediated glycal migration, and selective O- or C-migration under acidic or basic conditions.
